q: What does LAMA stand for in hospital discharge procedures? / अस्पताल की छुट्टी की प्रक्रिया में LAMA का क्या अर्थ है?
o: Leave Against Medical Advice | सलाह के विरुद्ध छोड़ना
e: LAMA refers to a patient choosing to leave the hospital despite the doctor's recommendation to stay for treatment. [cite: 81]

q: Which site is most commonly used to assess the pulse in an adult during a routine checkup? / नियमित जांच के दौरान वयस्क में पल्स मापने के लिए किस स्थान का सबसे अधिक उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: The radial artery at the wrist is the most common site for assessing pulse rate. [cite: 92]

q: What is the normal range for adult body temperature measured orally? / मौखिक रूप से मापे गए वयस्क शरीर के तापमान की सामान्य सीमा क्या है?
e: 98.6°F is considered the standard normal body temperature for a healthy adult. [cite: 90]

q: What is 'Autonomy' in nursing ethics? / नर्सिंग नैतिकता में 'स्वायत्तता' (Autonomy) क्या है?
o: The patient's right to make their own decisions | निर्णय लेने का रोगी का अधिकार
e: Autonomy respects the patient's right to self-determination regarding their medical care.

q: The principle of 'Beneficence' means: / 'बेनिफिशेंस' (Beneficence) के सिद्धांत का अर्थ है:
o: Doing good and acting in the patient's best interest | रोगी के हित में कार्य करना
e: Beneficence requires nurses to take positive actions to help others and ensure patient safety.

q: What is 'Non-maleficence'? / 'नॉन-मलेफिशेंस' (Non-maleficence) क्या है?
o: Duty to do no harm | हानि न पहुँचाने का कर्तव्य
e: This core principle dictates that a nurse must avoid actions that intentionally cause harm to the patient.

q: What is 'Professional Etiquette' in nursing? / नर्सिंग में 'पेशेवर शिष्टाचार' (Professional Etiquette) क्या है?
o: Rules for polite and professional behavior | विनम्र और पेशेवर व्यवहार के नियम
e: Etiquette includes proper communication, respect for colleagues, and maintaining professional boundaries.

q: In legal terms, 'Negligence' refers to: / कानूनी शब्दों में, 'लापरवाही' (Negligence) का तात्पर्य है:
o: Failure to provide a standard level of care | देखभाल के मानक स्तर में विफलता
e: Negligence occurs when a nurse's conduct falls below the expected professional standard, leading to injury.

q: What is 'Confidentiality'? / 'गोपनीयता' (Confidentiality) क्या है?
o: Protecting the patient's private information | रोगी की निजी जानकारी की रक्षा करना
e: Patient information should only be shared with the healthcare team directly involved in their care.

q: What is 'Informed Consent'? / 'सूचित सहमति' (Informed Consent) क्या है?
o: Asking the patient to sign a blank paper
Agreement after understanding the risks and benefits | जोखिम और लाभ समझने के बाद सहमति
e: Consent must be voluntary and based on a full understanding of the procedure or treatment.

q: What is the primary responsibility of a nurse toward a dying patient? / मरते हुए रोगी के प्रति नर्स की प्राथमिक जिम्मेदारी क्या है?
o: Providing comfort, dignity, and symptom management | आराम, गरिमा और लक्षण प्रबंधन
e: End-of-life care focuses on quality of life and supporting the patient and family emotionally.

q: The term 'Fidelity' in ethics refers to: / नैतिकता में 'फिडेलिटी' (Fidelity) शब्द का तात्पर्य है:
o: Being faithful and keeping promises | वफादार होना और वादे निभाना
e: Fidelity involves loyalty and fulfilling professional obligations to the patient.

q: What is 'Veracity'? / 'वेरासिटी' (Veracity) क्या है?
o: Truth-telling and honesty | सच बोलना और ईमानदारी
e: Veracity is essential for building a trusting relationship between the nurse and the patient.

q: What is 'Autonomy' in nursing ethics? / नर्सिंग नैतिकता में 'स्वायत्तता' (Autonomy) क्या है?

e: Autonomy respects the patient's right to self-determination regarding their medical care.

o: Truth-telling and honesty | सच बोलना और ईमानदारी
e: Veracity is essential for building a trusting relationship between the nurse and the patient.

q: What is 'Parenteral' nutrition? / 'पैरेंट्रल' (Parenteral) पोषण क्या है?
o: Nutrition given intravenously (via IV) | नसों के माध्यम से पोषण
e: It is used when the digestive system cannot absorb nutrients or must be bypassed.

q: Which electrolyte imbalance is called 'Hyperkalemia'? / किस इलेक्ट्रोलाइट असंतुलन को 'हाइपरकेलेमिया' कहा जाता है?
e: Hyperkalemia can cause serious heart rhythm problems and is a medical emergency.

q: What is the calorie value of 1 gram of 'Protein'? / 1 ग्राम 'प्रोटीन' का कैलोरी मान क्या है?
e: Both carbohydrates and proteins provide 4 kcal per gram, while fats provide 9 kcal.

q: A patient with NG tube feeding should be kept in which position to prevent aspiration? / एस्पिरेशन रोकने के लिए NG ट्यूब फीडिंग वाले मरीज को किस स्थिति में रखना चाहिए?
e: Elevating the head of the bed prevents stomach contents from flowing back into the lungs.

q: Which mineral imbalance is the primary cause of 'Tetany'? / 'टेटनी' (Tetany) का प्राथमिक कारण किस खनिज का असंतुलन है?
e: Hypocalcemia causes over-excitability of nerves and muscles, leading to cramps and tetany.

q: According to BMI, a person with a score of 30 or above is considered: / BMI के अनुसार, 30 या उससे अधिक स्कोर वाले व्यक्ति को माना जाता है:
e: A BMI of 18.5-24.9 is normal; 25-29.9 is overweight; and 30+ is obese.

q: What is 'Respiratory Acidosis' typically caused by? / 'रेस्पिरेटरी एसिडोसिस' आमतौर पर किसके कारण होता है?
e: When lungs cannot remove enough CO2, the blood becomes too acidic.

q: Which nutrient should be restricted in a patient with 'Hepatic Encephalopathy'? / 'हिपेटिक एन्सेफैलोपैथी' के रोगी में किस पोषक तत्व को सीमित किया जाना चाहिए?
e: Protein breakdown produces ammonia; reducing protein helps prevent ammonia buildup in the brain.

q: What is the purpose of 'Gavage' feeding? / 'गैवेज' (Gavage) फीडिंग का उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: Giving liquid nourishment via a tube to the stomach | ट्यूब से भोजन देना
e: Gavage is used for patients who cannot swallow, like premature infants or unconscious adults.

q: Which electrolyte is mainly found in 'Extracellular Fluid' (ECF)? / 'एक्स्ट्रासेलुलर फ्लूइड' (ECF) में मुख्य रूप से कौन सा इलेक्ट्रोलाइट पाया जाता है?
e: Sodium is the primary cation of the ECF, while Potassium is primary in the Intracellular Fluid.

q: Which vitamin is also known as the 'Sunshine Vitamin'? / किस विटामिन को 'सनशाइन विटामिन' के रूप में भी जाना जाता है?
e: The body produces Vitamin D when the skin is exposed to sunlight.

q: What is the main function of 'Vitamin K'? / 'विटामिन K' का मुख्य कार्य क्या है?
e: Vitamin K is essential for the synthesis of proteins needed for blood clotting.

q: Which mineral deficiency is linked to 'Goitre' (swelling of thyroid)? / किस खनिज की कमी 'घेंघा' (Goitre) से जुड़ी है?
e: Iodine is required for thyroid hormone production; its lack causes thyroid enlargement.

q: What is 'Anorexia'? / 'एनोरेक्सिया' (Anorexia) क्या है?
o: Loss of appetite | भूख न लगना
e: Anorexia can be a symptom of illness or a psychological eating disorder.

q: Which diet is recommended for a patient with 'Peptic Ulcer'? / 'पेप्टिक अल्सर' के रोगी के लिए कौन सा आहार अनुशंसित है?
e: A bland diet avoids spicy, fried, and acidic foods that irritate the stomach lining.

q: Deficiency of 'Iron' during pregnancy primarily increases the risk of: / गर्भावस्था के दौरान 'आयरन' की कमी मुख्य रूप से किसका खतरा बढ़ाती है?
e: Anemia can lead to preterm birth, low birth weight, and maternal exhaustion.

q: Which nutrient provides the most 'Calories' per gram? / प्रति ग्राम सबसे अधिक 'कैलोरी' कौन सा पोषक तत्व प्रदान करता है?
e: Fats provide 9 kcal per gram, more than double that of carbs or proteins (4 kcal).

q: Which food is a rich source of 'Vitamin B12'? / कौन सा भोजन 'विटामिन B12' का समृद्ध स्रोत है?
o: Animal products (Meat, Milk, Eggs) | पशु उत्पाद
e: B12 is found almost exclusively in animal-based foods; vegetarians may need supplements.

q: What is 'Clear Liquid Diet'? / 'क्लियर लिक्विड डाइट' क्या है?
o: Transparent liquids like water and broth | पारदर्शी तरल पदार्थ
e: It is used post-surgery to provide fluids without stressing the digestive system.

q: Which vitamin deficiency leads to 'Pernicious Anemia'? / किस विटामिन की कमी से 'पर्निसियस एनीमिया' होता है?
e: B12 is necessary for RBC formation; its deficiency leads to a specific type of anemia.

q: Which blood vessel carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs? / कौन सी रक्त वाहिका हृदय से फेफड़ों तक अशुद्ध रक्त ले जाती है?
e: Unlike other arteries, the pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood to the lungs for oxygenation.

q: What is the largest organ in the human body? / मानव शरीर का सबसे बड़ा अंग कौन सा है?
e: The skin is the largest organ by surface area and weight, providing protection and regulation.

q: Which type of immunity is transferred from mother to fetus through the placenta? / प्लेसेंटा के माध्यम से माँ से भ्रूण में कौन सी प्रतिरक्षा स्थानांतरित होती है?
o: Natural Passive Immunity | प्राकृतिक निष्क्रिय प्रतिरक्षा
e: Passive immunity involves the transfer of pre-formed antibodies (IgG) from mother to baby.

q: What is the function of 'Platelets' in the blood? / रक्त में 'प्लेटलेट्स' का क्या कार्य है?
o: Blood clotting | रक्त का थक्का जमना
e: Platelets (thrombocytes) are essential for stopping bleeding through the formation of clots.

q: Which acid is naturally present in the stomach to aid digestion? / पाचन में सहायता के लिए पेट में प्राकृतिक रूप से कौन सा एसिड मौजूद होता है?
e: HCl creates an acidic environment for enzymes like pepsin to break down proteins.

q: The process of 'Pasteurization' is commonly used for: / 'पाश्चुरीकरण' की प्रक्रिया आमतौर पर किसके लिए उपयोग की जाती है?
e: Pasteurization uses controlled heat to kill pathogens in milk and other beverages without affecting quality.

q: Which hormone regulates the 'Sleep-Wake' cycle? / कौन सा हार्मोन 'नींद-जागने' के चक्र को नियंत्रित करता है?
e: Melatonin is produced by the pineal gland in response to darkness, aiding sleep.

q: In Microbiology, 'Culture' refers to: / माइक्रोबायोलॉजी में, 'कल्चर' का तात्पर्य है:
o: Growth of microorganisms in a nutrient medium | पोषक माध्यम में सूक्ष्मजीवों की वृद्धि
e: A culture is used to multiply and identify specific pathogens from clinical samples.

q: Where does the exchange of gases occur in the lungs? / फेफड़ों में गैसों का आदान-प्रदान कहाँ होता है?
e: Alveoli are tiny air sacs where oxygen enters the blood and CO2 leaves it.

q: What is the normal pH of human blood? / मानव रक्त का सामान्य pH क्या है?
e: Blood is slightly alkaline; maintaining this narrow pH range is vital for survival.

q: Deficiency of which Vitamin causes 'Night Blindness'? / किस विटामिन की कमी से 'रतौंधी' (Night Blindness) होती है?
e: Vitamin A is essential for the production of rhodopsin, necessary for vision in low light.

q: Which nutrient is the primary source of 'Energy' for the human body? / मानव शरीर के लिए 'ऊर्जा' का प्राथमिक स्रोत कौन सा पोषक तत्व है?
e: Carbohydrates provide about 4 kcal per gram and are the body's preferred energy source.

q: Scurvy is caused by the deficiency of: / स्कर्वी (Scurvy) किसकी कमी के कारण होता है?
e: Vitamin C is vital for collagen synthesis; its lack leads to bleeding gums and skin spots.

q: Which mineral is necessary for the formation of 'Hemoglobin'? / 'हीमोग्लोबिन' के निर्माण के लिए कौन सा खनिज आवश्यक है?
e: Iron is a core component of hemoglobin, which carries oxygen in the blood.

q: What is 'Balanced Diet'? / 'संतुलित आहार' क्या है?
o: Diet containing all essential nutrients in correct proportions | सभी पोषक तत्वों वाला आहार
e: A balanced diet provides proteins, fats, carbs, vitamins, and minerals required for optimal health.

q: Which disease is caused by extreme 'Protein-Energy Malnutrition' (PEM)? / 'प्रोटीन-ऊर्जा कुपोषण' (PEM) के कारण कौन सी बीमारी होती है?
o: Kwashiorkor and Marasmus | क्वाशियोरकोर और मारस्मस
e: Kwashiorkor results from protein deficiency, while Marasmus is a deficiency of total calories.

q: What is the recommended diet for a patient with 'Hypertension'? / 'उच्च रक्तचाप' के रोगी के लिए अनुशंसित आहार क्या है?
o: DASH diet (Low Sodium) | कम नमक वाला आहार
e: Reducing sodium and increasing potassium helps lower blood pressure.

q: Rickets in children is caused by the deficiency of: / बच्चों में रिकेट्स (Rickets) किसकी कमी से होता है?
e: Vitamin D is essential for calcium absorption; its deficiency leads to soft, weak bones.

q: Which food group is most important for 'Tissue Repair' and growth? / 'ऊतक मरम्मत' और विकास के लिए कौन सा खाद्य समूह सबसे महत्वपूर्ण है?
e: Proteins are the building blocks of the body, necessary for repairing tissues and building muscle.

q: Which diet is prescribed for a patient with 'Chronic Kidney Disease'? / 'क्रोनिक किडनी डिजीज' के रोगी के लिए कौन सा आहार निर्धारित किया जाता है?
o: Low protein and restricted potassium diet | कम प्रोटीन वाला आहार
e: Limiting protein and minerals reduces the workload on failing kidneys.

q: Which organ is known as the 'Powerhouse' of the cell? / कोशिका का 'पावरहाउस' किस अंग को कहा जाता है?
e: Mitochondria are responsible for generating ATP, the energy currency of the cell.

q: What is the total number of bones in an adult human body? / एक वयस्क मानव शरीर में हड्डियों की कुल संख्या कितनी होती है?
e: An adult human skeleton consists of 206 bones, while infants have more bones that fuse as they grow.

q: Which method of sterilization uses steam under pressure? / दबाव में भाप का उपयोग करने वाली कीटाणुशोधन विधि कौन सी है?
e: Autoclaving (121°C for 15-20 mins) is the most reliable method for destroying all microorganisms including spores.

q: Bacteria that require oxygen for growth are called: / वृद्धि के लिए ऑक्सीजन की आवश्यकता वाले बैक्टीरिया कहलाते हैं:
e: Aerobic bacteria need an oxygen-rich environment to survive and multiply.

q: Which part of the brain controls balance and coordination? / मस्तिष्क का कौन सा हिस्सा संतुलन और समन्वय को नियंत्रित करता है?
e: The cerebellum is responsible for maintaining posture, balance, and motor coordination.

q: What is the 'Master Gland' of the endocrine system? / अंतःस्रावी तंत्र की 'मास्टर ग्रंथि' कौन सी है?
e: The pituitary gland controls the functions of many other endocrine glands.

q: Gram staining is used to differentiate: / ग्राम स्टेनिंग का उपयोग किसे अलग करने के लिए किया जाता है?
e: Gram staining identifies bacteria based on the chemical and physical properties of their cell walls.

q: Which gas is used for chemical sterilization of heat-sensitive items? / गर्मी के प्रति संवेदनशील वस्तुओं के रासायनिक कीटाणुशोधन के लिए किस गैस का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Ethylene Oxide (ETO) is widely used for sterilizing surgical instruments and plastics that cannot withstand heat.

q: The structural and functional unit of the kidney is: / गुर्दे की संरचनात्मक और कार्यात्मक इकाई है:
e: Nephrons filter the blood and form urine.

q: Which type of microorganism causes 'Malaria'? / किस प्रकार का सूक्ष्मजीव 'मलेरिया' का कारण बनता है?
e: Malaria is caused by the Plasmodium parasite, a type of protozoa transmitted by mosquitoes.

q: What is the full form of 'NRHM'? / 'NRHM' का पूर्ण रूप क्या है?
o: National Rural Health Mission | राष्ट्रीय ग्रामीण स्वास्थ्य मिशन
e: NRHM was launched to provide accessible, affordable, and quality health care to the rural population.

q: Who is the 'Village Level' health worker under NRHM? / NRHM के तहत 'ग्राम स्तर' का स्वास्थ्य कार्यकर्ता कौन है?
e: Accredited Social Health Activists (ASHA) act as a bridge between the community and the health system.

q: What is 'ORS' therapy primarily used for in the community? / समुदाय में 'ORS' थेरेपी मुख्य रूप से किसके लिए उपयोग की जाती है?
o: Rehydration in Diarrhea | डायरिया में पुनर्जलीकरण
e: Oral Rehydration Salts are used to prevent and treat dehydration caused by diarrhea.

q: What is the 'Eligible Couple' in family planning? / परिवार नियोजन में 'योग्य जोड़ा' (Eligible Couple) क्या है?
o: Married couple where the woman is in the reproductive age (15-45) | प्रजनन आयु वाला जोड़ा
e: Eligible couples are those who are currently in the reproductive age group and need family planning services.

q: Which organization is responsible for the 'Mid-day Meal' program globally? / वैश्विक स्तर पर 'मिड-डे मील' कार्यक्रम के लिए कौन सा संगठन जिम्मेदार है?
e: Organizations like UNICEF and World Food Programme support nutritional initiatives for children.

q: What is the primary sign of 'Filaria' in the chronic stage? / पुराने चरण में 'फाइलेरिया' (Filaria) का प्राथमिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Elephantiasis (Swelling of limbs) | हाथीपाँव (पैरों की सूजन)
e: Lymphatic Filariasis leads to chronic swelling, most commonly in the legs and scrotum.

q: What is 'Demography'? / 'जनसांख्यिकी' (Demography) क्या है?
o: Scientific study of human populations | मानव जनसंख्या का वैज्ञानिक अध्ययन
e: It involves the study of population size, structure, distribution, and changes over time.

q: Which vaccine is administered as a 'Subcutaneous' injection for Measles? / खसरे के लिए किस टीके को 'सबक्यूटिनियस' इंजेक्शन के रूप में लगाया जाता है?
e: The measles vaccine is typically given subcutaneously in the right upper arm.

q: What is the 'Pre-pathogenesis' period? / 'प्री-पैथोजेनेसिस' (Pre-pathogenesis) अवधि क्या है?
o: Process before the disease agent enters the human host | शरीर में प्रवेश से पहले की प्रक्रिया
e: It is the period where the interaction between the agent, host, and environment occurs before the disease begins.

q: Which index is used to measure the 'Obesity' in a community survey? / सामुदायिक सर्वेक्षण में 'मोटापा' मापने के लिए किस सूचकांक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: BMI is a simple index of weight-for-height that is commonly used to classify underweight, overweight, and obesity.

q: What is the 'Incubation Period' of Measles? / खसरे (Measles) की 'इनक्यूबेशन अवधि' क्या है?
e: The incubation period for measles typically ranges from 10 to 14 days from exposure to the onset of fever.

q: Which of the following is a 'Zoonotic' disease? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी एक 'ज़ूनोटिक' (Zoonotic) बीमारी है?
e: Zoonotic diseases are those that are transmitted from animals to humans, such as Rabies via animal bites.

q: What is the primary mode of transmission for 'Hepatitis A'? / 'हेपेटाइटिस A' के संचरण का प्राथमिक तरीका क्या है?
e: Hepatitis A is primarily spread through contaminated food and water (fecal-oral route).

q: In Epidemiology, what does 'Pandemic' refer to? / महामारी विज्ञान में, 'पैंडेमिक' (Pandemic) का क्या अर्थ है?
o: Worldwide spread of a new disease | विश्वव्यापी प्रसार
e: A pandemic is an epidemic occurring worldwide, or over a very wide area, crossing international boundaries.

q: Which diagnostic test is used for 'Typhoid' fever? / 'टाइफाइड' बुखार के लिए किस नैदानिक परीक्षण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: The Widal test measures agglutinating antibodies against Salmonella typhi in the patient's serum.

q: What is 'Chemoprophylaxis'? / 'कीमोप्रोफिलैक्सिस' (Chemoprophylaxis) क्या है?
o: Administration of drugs to prevent the onset of a disease | बीमारी रोकने के लिए दवा देना
e: It involves giving medications to healthy individuals to prevent them from developing a specific infection.

q: Which vitamin deficiency is specifically targeted in the national prophylaxis program to prevent blindness? / अंधापन रोकने के लिए राष्ट्रीय कार्यक्रम में किस विटामिन की कमी को लक्षित किया गया है?
e: Massive doses of Vitamin A are given to children to prevent Xerophthalmia and nutritional blindness.

q: What is the main vector for 'Kala-azar'? / 'काला-अजार' का मुख्य वाहक (Vector) क्या है?
e: Kala-azar (Visceral Leishmaniasis) is transmitted by the bite of infected female phlebotomine sandflies.

q: Which level of prevention includes 'Early Diagnosis and Prompt Treatment'? / 'शीघ्र निदान और तत्काल उपचार' बचाव के किस स्तर में शामिल है?
e: Secondary prevention focuses on early detection of disease and intervention to prevent complications.

q: What is the causative agent of 'Syphilis'? / 'सिफलिस' का कारण बनने वाला एजेंट कौन सा है?
e: Syphilis is a sexually transmitted infection caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum.

q: What is the 'Window Period' in HIV infection? / HIV संक्रमण में 'विंडो पीरियड' क्या है?
o: Time between infection and appearance of antibodies | संक्रमण और एंटीबॉडी के बीच का समय
e: During this period, a person is infected but the blood test may show a negative result.

q: Which test is used for the screening of 'HIV'? / 'HIV' की स्क्रीनिंग के लिए किस टेस्ट का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: ELISA is the primary screening test, followed by Western Blot for confirmation.

q: What is the mode of transmission for 'Malaria'? / 'मलेरिया' के संचरण का तरीका क्या है?
o: Bite of infected Female Anopheles mosquito | मादा एनाफिलीज मच्छर का काटना
e: The parasite Plasmodium is injected into the bloodstream via the mosquito's saliva.

q: In Epidemiology, what does 'Endemic' mean? / महामारी विज्ञान में, 'एंडेमिक' (Endemic) का क्या अर्थ है?
o: Constant presence of a disease in a specific area | किसी क्षेत्र में बीमारी की निरंतर उपस्थिति
e: An endemic disease is always present at a baseline level in a particular population or region.

q: Which is the drug of choice for 'Cholera' to reduce the duration of illness? / बीमारी की अवधि कम करने के लिए 'हैजा' की दवा कौन सी है?
e: While ORS is primary for hydration, antibiotics help clear the Vibrio cholerae bacteria faster.

q: What is the 'DOTS' strategy used for? / 'DOTS' रणनीति का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
e: Directly Observed Treatment, Short-course ensures patient compliance for TB treatment.

q: Which organization provides global leadership in public health? / सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य में वैश्विक नेतृत्व कौन सा संगठन प्रदान करता है?
e: WHO sets international health standards and monitors global disease outbreaks.

q: What is the 'Cold Chain' system? / 'कोल्ड चेन' (Cold Chain) सिस्टम क्या है?
o: System of transporting and storing vaccines at low temperatures | टीकों के भंडारण की व्यवस्था
e: It is critical for maintaining the potency of temperature-sensitive vaccines.

q: Which symptom is characteristic of 'Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever'? / 'डेंगू रक्तस्रावी बुखार' का विशिष्ट लक्षण क्या है?
o: Decrease in platelet count and bleeding | प्लेटलेट काउंट में कमी
e: Thrombocytopenia (low platelets) leads to severe bleeding risks in dengue patients.

q: What is 'Quarantine'? / 'क्वारंटाइन' (Quarantine) क्या है?
o: Restriction of movement for healthy people exposed to infection | स्वस्थ लोगों की आवाजाही पर प्रतिबंध
e: Quarantine is for people who were exposed but are not yet showing symptoms, unlike isolation for the sick.

q: What is the 'Primary' level of prevention? / बचाव का 'प्राथमिक' (Primary) स्तर क्या है?
o: Health promotion and specific protection | स्वास्थ्य संवर्धन और विशिष्ट सुरक्षा
e: Primary prevention aims to prevent the onset of disease through measures like immunization and nutrition.

q: Which method is most effective for disinfecting water at the household level? / घरेलू स्तर पर पानी को कीटाणुमुक्त करने के लिए कौन सी विधि सबसे प्रभावी है?
e: Boiling water is the simplest and most effective way to kill pathogenic microorganisms.

q: What is the main objective of 'Health Education'? / 'स्वास्थ्य शिक्षा' का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: To change people's behavior towards healthy living | व्यवहार में बदलाव
e: Health education empowers individuals to take responsibility for their own health through informed choices.

q: Which vector is responsible for spreading 'Dengue' fever? / 'डेंगू' बुखार फैलाने के लिए कौन सा वाहक जिम्मेदार है?
o: Aedes aegypti mosquito | एडीज इजिप्टी मच्छर
e: Aedes aegypti is a day-biting mosquito that transmits the dengue virus.

q: The concept of 'Social Distance' is primarily used to control: / 'सोशल डिस्टेंस' की अवधारणा मुख्य रूप से किसे नियंत्रित करने के लिए उपयोग की जाती है?
e: Physical distancing prevents the spread of pathogens through respiratory droplets or direct contact.

q: What is 'Epidemiology'? / 'महामारी विज्ञान' (Epidemiology) क्या है?
o: Study of the distribution and determinants of diseases | बीमारियों के वितरण का अध्ययन
e: It is the fundamental science of public health used to identify risk factors and disease patterns.

q: Which gas is primarily responsible for 'Global Warming'? / 'ग्लोबल वार्मिंग' के लिए मुख्य रूप से कौन सी गैस जिम्मेदार है?
e: CO2 acts as a greenhouse gas, trapping heat in the Earth's atmosphere.

q: What is the ideal 'Incubation Period' of Cholera? / हैजा (Cholera) की आदर्श 'इनक्यूबेशन अवधि' क्या है?
o: A few hours to 5 days | कुछ घंटे से 5 दिन
e: Cholera has a very short incubation period, leading to rapid outbreaks.

q: Which of the following is a 'Water-borne' disease? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी 'जल-जनित' (Water-borne) बीमारी है?
e: Typhoid fever is spread through contaminated food and water.

q: What does 'Somatic' health education focus on? / 'सोमैटिक' स्वास्थ्य शिक्षा किस पर ध्यान केंद्रित करती है?
o: Physical health of the body | शरीर का भौतिक स्वास्थ्य
e: It deals with hygiene, exercise, and physical well-being.

q: What is the defining feature of 'Obsession' in OCD? / OCD में 'जुनून' (Obsession) की परिभाषित विशेषता क्या है?
o: Recurrent, intrusive, and unwanted thoughts | बार-बार आने वाले अनचाहे विचार
e: Obsessions are the mental component of OCD that cause significant anxiety.

q: What is 'Systematic Desensitization' used for? / 'सिस्टमैटिक डीसेंसिटाइजेशन' का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
e: It is a behavioral therapy where the patient is gradually exposed to the feared object while relaxed.

q: In 'Seclusion', the nurse's primary responsibility is: / 'अलगाव' (Seclusion) में नर्स की प्राथमिक जिम्मेदारी है:
o: Continuous safety monitoring and meeting basic needs | सुरक्षा निगरानी और बुनियादी जरूरतें
e: Safety and ethical care are paramount when a patient is isolated for behavioral control.

q: What is 'Acrophobia'? / 'एक्रोफोबिया' (Acrophobia) क्या है?
o: Fear of heights | ऊंचाई से डर
e: Acrophobia is an irrational and persistent fear of heights.

q: What is the focus of 'Exposure and Response Prevention' (ERP) for OCD? / OCD के लिए 'एक्सपोज़र और रिस्पांस प्रिवेंशन' (ERP) का केंद्र क्या है?
o: Facing the fear without performing the ritual | बिना क्रिया किए डर का सामना करना
e: ERP helps break the cycle of anxiety by preventing the compulsive behavior used to relieve it.

q: Which nursing intervention is best for a patient with 'Agoraphobia'? / 'अगोराफोबिया' वाले मरीज के लिए कौन सा नर्सिंग हस्तक्षेप सबसे अच्छा है?
o: Gradual exposure to outdoor environments | बाहरी वातावरण का क्रमिक सामना
Keep them in a dark room
e: Gradual exposure helps the patient build confidence in spaces where they feel trapped.

q: What is 'Nyctophobia'? / 'नाइक्टोफोबिया' (Nyctophobia) क्या है?
o: Fear of darkness/night | अंधेरे या रात से डर
e: It is an extreme fear of the night or darkness.

q: When should 'Physical Restraints' be removed? / 'शारीरिक प्रतिबंध' कब हटाए जाने चाहिए?
o: As soon as the patient is safe and regains control | जैसे ही मरीज सुरक्षित हो जाए
e: Restraints must be discontinued at the earliest possible time to protect the patient's rights.

q: A patient with OCD spends hours washing hands. The nurse should: / OCD वाला मरीज घंटों हाथ धोता है। नर्स को चाहिए:
o: Establish a structured schedule to gradually reduce time | समय को धीरे-धीरे कम करने के लिए सारणी बनाना
e: Abruptly stopping the ritual can cause severe panic; a structured approach is more therapeutic.

q: The patient's right to 'Informed Consent' means: / रोगी के 'सूचित सहमति' के अधिकार का अर्थ है:
o: Understanding risks and benefits before treatment | उपचार से पहले जोखिम और लाभ को समझना
e: Informed consent is a legal and ethical requirement ensuring the patient knows what they are agreeing to.

q: Which therapy uses a system of 'Rewards' for good behavior? / किस थेरेपी में अच्छे व्यवहार के लिए 'इनाम' की प्रणाली का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Token economy is a form of behavior modification where positive behavior is rewarded with tokens.

q: What is 'Aversion Therapy'? / 'एवर्जन थेरेपी' (Aversion Therapy) क्या है?
o: Associating an undesirable habit with an unpleasant stimulus | अवांछित आदत को अप्रिय अनुभव से जोड़ना
e: It is often used to treat alcoholism or smoking by linking the habit with something unpleasant (like a mild shock or nausea-inducing drug).

q: In 'Psychoanalysis', who is the founder? / 'मनोविश्लेषण' (Psychoanalysis) के संस्थापक कौन हैं?
e: Freud developed psychoanalysis focusing on the unconscious mind and childhood experiences.

q: What is 'Catatonia'? / 'कैटेटोनिया' (Catatonia) क्या है?
o: Abnormality of movement and behavior arising from a disturbed mental state | शारीरिक जड़ता
e: Catatonia can involve complete lack of movement (stupor) or rigid body posture for long periods.

q: Under the Mental Healthcare Act 2017, 'Suicide' is: / मानसिक स्वास्थ्य देखभाल अधिनियम 2017 के तहत, 'आत्महत्या' है:
o: Decriminalized (presumed to be under severe stress) | अपराध की श्रेणी से बाहर
e: The law now presumes that a person attempting suicide is under severe stress and should be treated, not punished.

q: What is 'Empathy' in nursing practice? / नर्सिंग अभ्यास में 'सहानुभूति' (Empathy) क्या है?
o: Understanding the patient's feelings from their perspective | रोगी की भावनाओं को उनके नजरिए से समझना
e: Empathy is a core skill in therapeutic communication where the nurse understands but remains professional.

q: Which side effect is common with first-generation Antipsychotics? / पहली पीढ़ी की एंटीसाइकोटिक्स दवाओं का सामान्य दुष्प्रभाव क्या है?
e: EPS includes muscle spasms, tremors, and restlessness (Akathisia).

q: What is 'Waxy Flexibility'? / 'वैक्सी फ्लेक्सिबिलिटी' (Waxy Flexibility) क्या है?
o: Patient maintains a body position for a long time as if made of wax | शरीर का एक ही स्थिति में बने रहना
e: It is a sign often seen in catatonic schizophrenia.

q: A patient feels that 'everyone is plotting against them'. This is: / एक मरीज को लगता है कि 'सब उसके खिलाफ साजिश कर रहे हैं'। यह है:
o: Delusion of Persecution | उत्पीड़न का भ्रम
e: This is a common symptom in paranoid disorders where the patient is extremely suspicious.

q: What is 'Hypochondriasis'? / 'हाइपोकॉन्ड्रियासिस' (Hypochondriasis) क्या है?
o: Abnormal anxiety about one's health, often believing they have a serious disease | बीमारी का काल्पनिक डर
e: The patient is preoccupied with the fear of having a serious illness despite medical reassurance.

q: What is the primary symptom of 'Schizophrenia'? / 'सिज़ोफ्रेनिया' (Schizophrenia) का प्राथमिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Distorted thinking and social withdrawal | विकृत सोच और सामाजिक अलगाव
e: Schizophrenia is a severe mental disorder where people interpret reality abnormally, often involving delusions and hallucinations.

q: Which of the following is a symptom of 'Alcohol Withdrawal'? / 'अल्कोहल विड्रॉल' (शराब छोड़ने के लक्षण) का लक्षण कौन सा है?
o: Tremors and agitation | कंपन और घबराहट
e: Withdrawal symptoms occur when a person suddenly stops heavy drinking, leading to physical and mental distress.

q: A patient repeats the same word or phrase again and again. This is called: / एक मरीज बार-बार एक ही शब्द या वाक्यांश दोहराता है। इसे कहा जाता है:
e: Echolalia is the meaningless repetition of another person's spoken words as a symptom of a psychiatric disorder.

q: What is 'Anorexia Nervosa'? / 'एनोरेक्सिया नर्वोसा' (Anorexia Nervosa) क्या है?
o: Refusal to eat due to fear of gaining weight | वजन बढ़ने के डर से खाना न खाना
e: It is an eating disorder characterized by an abnormally low body weight and intense fear of gaining weight.

q: Which medication is an 'Antidote' for Benzodiazepine overdose? / बेंजोडायजेपाइन ओवरडोज के लिए 'एंटीडोट' कौन सा है?
e: Flumazenil is used to reverse the sedative effects of benzodiazepines in emergency cases.

q: What is 'Pica'? / 'पिका' (Pica) क्या है?
o: Eating non-food substances like mud or chalk | अखाद्य पदार्थों को खाना
e: Pica is a disorder where people eat items that have no nutritional value, like dirt, hair, or paper.

q: What is the nurse's priority for a patient in 'Acute Mania'? / 'एक्यूट मेनिया' वाले मरीज के लिए नर्स की प्राथमिकता क्या है?
o: Reducing environmental stimuli and ensuring safety | उत्तेजना को कम करना और सुरक्षा
e: Manic patients are easily overstimulated; a quiet environment helps stabilize their mood.

q: A 'False sensory perception' with no real basis is: / बिना किसी वास्तविक आधार के 'झूठी संवेदी धारणा' है:
e: Unlike illusion (misinterpretation), hallucination has no external stimulus at all.

q: Which vitamin deficiency is common in chronic alcoholism? / पुराने शराब के सेवन में किस विटामिन की कमी आम है?
e: Thiamine deficiency can lead to Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome, a serious brain disorder.

q: What is 'Bulimia Nervosa'? / 'बुलिमिया नर्वोसा' (Bulimia Nervosa) क्या है?
o: Binge eating followed by purging (vomiting) | अत्यधिक खाना और फिर उल्टी करना
e: It is an eating disorder where a person eats large amounts of food and then tries to get rid of it in unhealthy ways.

q: What is 'Electroconvulsive Therapy' (ECT)? / 'इलेक्ट्रोकन्वल्सिव थेरेपी' (ECT) क्या है?
o: Passing small electric currents to induce seizures | बिजली के झटके से उपचार
e: ECT is used for severe depression or schizophrenia when medications are not effective.

q: What is a mandatory requirement before starting ECT? / ECT शुरू करने से पहले एक अनिवार्य आवश्यकता क्या है?
e: The patient or their legal guardian must provide written consent after understanding the risks.

q: Which medication class is used to treat Schizophrenia? / सिज़ोफ्रेनिया के इलाज के लिए किस दवा वर्ग का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Antipsychotics help manage symptoms like delusions and hallucinations.

q: In 'Cognitive Behavioral Therapy' (CBT), the focus is on: / 'संज्ञानात्मक व्यवहार थेरेपी' (CBT) में ध्यान केंद्रित किया जाता है:
o: Changing negative thought patterns | नकारात्मक विचारों को बदलना
e: CBT helps patients identify and change distorted thinking that leads to emotional distress.

q: What is the primary role of a nurse after ECT treatment? / ECT उपचार के बाद नर्स की प्राथमिक भूमिका क्या है?
o: Monitoring vitals and ensuring a safe airway | वाइटल और वायुमार्ग की निगरानी
e: The patient is often confused and at risk for aspiration immediately after the procedure.

q: Which drug is commonly used as a 'Mood Stabilizer' in Bipolar Disorder? / बाइपोलर डिसऑर्डर में 'मूड स्टेबलाइजर' के रूप में आमतौर पर किस दवा का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Lithium helps prevent both manic and depressive episodes.

q: What is 'Occupational Therapy' in psychiatry? / मनोरोग में 'ऑक्यूपेशनल थेरेपी' क्या है?
o: Using meaningful activities to improve daily functioning | सार्थक गतिविधियों का उपयोग
e: It helps patients regain skills needed for independent living.

q: What is 'Phobia'? / 'फोबिया' (Phobia) क्या है?
o: Persistent and irrational fear of a specific object | विशिष्ट डर
e: Examples include fear of heights (acrophobia) or enclosed spaces (claustrophobia).

q: Which therapy involves the patient interacting with others in a group setting? / किस थेरेपी में रोगी समूह में दूसरों के साथ बातचीत करता है?
e: Group therapy provides support and helps patients realize they are not alone in their struggles.

q: The nurse-patient relationship in psychiatry should be: / मनोरोग में नर्स-रोगी संबंध कैसा होना चाहिए?
o: Therapeutic and professional | चिकित्सीय और व्यावसायिक
e: A therapeutic relationship is built on trust, empathy, and professional boundaries.

q: What is the primary characteristic of 'Depression' in a psychiatric patient? / मनोरोग रोगी में 'अवसाद' (Depression) की प्राथमिक विशेषता क्या है?
o: Persistent sadness and loss of interest | लगातार उदासी और रुचि की कमी
e: Depression is characterized by anhedonia (inability to feel pleasure) and a persistent low mood.

q: Which nursing intervention is most critical for a 'Suicidal' patient? / 'आत्महत्या की प्रवृत्ति' वाले मरीज के लिए कौन सा नर्सिंग हस्तक्षेप सबसे महत्वपूर्ण है?
e: Safety is the priority; the patient must never be left alone to prevent self-harm.

q: What is 'Hallucination'? / 'मतिभ्रम' (Hallucination) क्या है?
o: Perception without any external stimuli | बिना किसी बाहरी उद्दीपन के धारणा
e: Hallucination involves seeing, hearing, or feeling things that are not actually present.

q: A patient believes they are a 'King' despite evidence to the contrary. This is called: / एक मरीज खुद को 'राजा' मानता है। इसे क्या कहा जाता है?
o: Delusion of Grandeur | भव्यता का भ्रम (Delusion)
e: Delusion is a fixed, false belief that is not shaken by logic or evidence.

q: Which of the following is a sign of 'Abnormal' behavior? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा 'असामान्य' व्यवहार का संकेत है?
o: Social withdrawal and self-neglect | सामाजिक अलगाव और खुद की उपेक्षा
e: Abnormal behavior often interferes with daily functioning and social relationships.

q: What is 'Anxiety'? / 'चिंता' (Anxiety) क्या है?
o: A feeling of apprehension or dread | घबराहट या डर की भावना
e: Anxiety is an emotional response to a perceived or real threat, often accompanied by physical symptoms.

q: In 'Mania', the patient typically shows: / 'मेनिया' (Mania) में, रोगी आमतौर पर क्या दिखाता है?
o: Elated mood and flight of ideas | उत्साहित मूड और विचारों की तेजी
e: Mania is the opposite of depression, characterized by excessive activity and euphoria.

q: What should a nurse do first if a patient expresses suicidal thoughts? / यदि कोई मरीज आत्मघाती विचार व्यक्त करता है तो नर्स को सबसे पहले क्या करना चाहिए?
o: Take it seriously and assess the plan | गंभीरता से लें और योजना का आकलन करें
e: All suicidal threats must be taken seriously and reported to the psychiatric team immediately.

q: What is 'Illusion'? / 'इल्यूजन' (Illusion) क्या है?
o: Misinterpretation of a real external stimulus | वास्तविक उद्दीपन की गलत व्याख्या
e: Example: Seeing a rope and thinking it is a snake.

q: The therapeutic environment in a psychiatric ward is often called: / मनोरोग वार्ड में चिकित्सीय वातावरण को अक्सर क्या कहा जाता है?
e: Milieu therapy uses the environment to promote safety and social learning.

q: Which of the following is a 'Cyanotic' congenital heart disease? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा एक 'सायनोटिक' जन्मजात हृदय रोग है?
o: Tetralogy of Fallot (TOF) | टेट्रालॉजी ऑफ फैलोट
e: TOF is a cyanotic heart defect where deoxygenated blood mixes with oxygenated blood, leading to a blue tint in the skin (cyanosis).

q: What is the hallmark sign of 'Kwashiorkor' that distinguishes it from Marasmus? / क्वाशियोरकोर का प्रमुख लक्षण क्या है जो इसे मारस्मस से अलग करता है?
e: Kwashiorkor is primarily a protein deficiency causing fluid retention (edema), whereas Marasmus is overall calorie deficiency causing severe wasting.

q: What is 'Play Therapy' in a hospital setting? / अस्पताल सेटिंग में 'प्ले थेरेपी' (Play Therapy) क्या है?
o: Using play to help children express feelings and cope with illness | खेल के माध्यम से तनाव कम करना
e: Play therapy is a therapeutic approach used by nurses to reduce a child's anxiety and help them understand medical procedures.

q: In 'Tetralogy of Fallot', what should a nurse do during a 'Cyanotic/Tet Spell'? / TOF में, 'सायनोटिक स्पेल' के दौरान नर्स को क्या करना चाहिए?
o: Place the child in Knee-Chest position | घुटने-छाती (Knee-Chest) स्थिति में रखें
e: The knee-chest position increases systemic vascular resistance, improving blood flow to the lungs during a spell.

q: Which condition is characterized by a 'Moon Face' appearance in children? / बच्चों में 'मून फेस' (Moon Face) किस स्थिति की विशेषता है?
e: In Kwashiorkor, edema and fatty liver can give the face a rounded, 'moon-like' appearance.

q: The 'Right to Protection from Abuse' falls under: / 'दुर्व्यवहार से सुरक्षा का अधिकार' किसके अंतर्गत आता है?
e: The UN Convention on the Rights of the Child ensures that every child is protected from all forms of physical or mental violence.

q: What is the primary nursing goal for a child with 'Spina Bifida' before surgery? / सर्जरी से पहले 'स्पाइना बिफिडा' वाले बच्चे के लिए प्राथमिक नर्सिंग लक्ष्य क्या है?
o: Protecting the sac from injury and infection | सैक को चोट और संक्रमण से बचाना
e: Preventing rupture of the protruding sac is vital to avoid life-threatening meningitis.

q: Which type of play is typical for 'Infants'? / 'शिशुओं' के लिए किस प्रकार का खेल विशिष्ट है?
e: Infants usually play alone (solitary) and are not interested in playing with other children yet.

q: In 'Marasmus', the child has an appearance often described as: / 'मारस्मस' में बच्चे का रूप अक्सर किस तरह वर्णित किया जाता है?
o: Old man's face (Wizened) | बूढ़े व्यक्ति जैसा चेहरा
e: Due to extreme loss of subcutaneous fat and muscle, the skin hangs loose, making the child look like an old person.

q: What is the focus of a 'Child Guidance Clinic' (CGC)? / 'चाइल्ड गाइडेंस क्लिनिक' (CGC) का केंद्र क्या है?
o: Preventive, curative, and rehabilitative mental health services | मानसिक स्वास्थ्य सेवाएं
e: CGCs address emotional, behavioral, and learning problems in children through a team of specialists.

q: At what age does a child typically start 'Parallel Play'? / एक बच्चा आमतौर पर किस उम्र में 'समानांतर खेल' (Parallel Play) शुरू करता है?
e: Toddlers often play side-by-side with other children but not directly with them. [cite: 194]

q: Which vaccine provides protection against 'Whooping Cough' (Pertussis)? / कौन सा टीका 'काली खांसी' (Pertussis) से सुरक्षा प्रदान करता है?
e: Pertussis is the 'P' component of the DPT vaccine. [cite: 154]

q: What is the most common cause of 'Accidental Poisoning' in toddlers? / टॉडलर्स में 'आकस्मिक विषाक्तता' का सबसे सामान्य कारण क्या है?
o: Unsafe storage of household chemicals | रसायनों का असुरक्षित भंडारण
e: Toddlers are curious and often ingest colorful medicines or chemicals kept within reach. [cite: 114, 194]

q: A child who can 'run, climb stairs, and use 2-3 word sentences' is likely: / एक बच्चा जो 'दौड़ सकता है, सीढ़ियां चढ़ सकता है और 2-3 शब्दों के वाक्य बोल सकता है' वह लगभग है:
e: These are typical developmental milestones for a two-year-old toddler. [cite: 57, 193]

q: Which immunization is given as a 'Booster' dose at 16-24 months? / 16-24 महीने की उम्र में 'बूस्टर' खुराक के रूप में कौन सा टीकाकरण दिया जाता है?
o: DPT and OPV Booster | डीपीटी और ओपीवी बूस्टर
e: Booster doses are vital to maintain immunity levels established by primary doses. [cite: 62, 195]

q: What is the significance of the 'Growth Chart'? / 'ग्रोथ चार्ट' का क्या महत्व है?
o: To monitor a child's growth over time and detect malnutrition | वृद्धि की निगरानी
e: It helps health workers identify deviations from normal growth patterns early. [cite: 43, 193]

q: Which position should a baby be placed in for 'Sleep' to prevent SIDS? / SIDS को रोकने के लिए बच्चे को 'सोने' के लिए किस पोजीशन में रखा जाना चाहिए?
o: On the back (Supine) | पीठ के बल
e: Back-to-sleep is the safest position recommended to reduce the risk of Sudden Infant Death Syndrome. [cite: 28, 62]

q: A deficiency of 'Vitamin A' in children can lead to: / बच्चों में 'विटामिन A' की कमी से क्या हो सकता है?
e: Vitamin A is crucial for vision; chronic deficiency can lead to permanent blindness. [cite: 15, 150]

q: What is 'Failure to Thrive' (FTT)? / 'फेलियर टू थ्राइव' (FTT) क्या है?
o: Inadequate weight gain or growth in children | अपर्याप्त शारीरिक वृद्धि
e: FTT can be due to medical issues, poor nutrition, or psychosocial neglect. [cite: 18, 57]

q: What is the primary role of a 'School Health Nurse'? / 'स्कूल हेल्थ नर्स' की प्राथमिक भूमिका क्या है?
o: Screening for health problems and health education | स्वास्थ्य स्क्रीनिंग
e: They ensure children are healthy, immunized, and educated about personal hygiene. [cite: 36, 62]

q: What is the primary sign of 'Respiratory Distress Syndrome' (RDS) in a newborn? / नवजात शिशु में 'रेस्पिरेटरी डिस्ट्रेस सिंड्रोम' (RDS) का प्राथमिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Nasal flaring and chest retractions | नाक फूलना और छाती का धंसना
e: RDS is common in preterm babies due to lack of surfactant, causing labored breathing and grunting. [cite: 198]

q: Which condition is a surgical emergency characterized by 'Current Jelly' stools? / 'करंट जेली' जैसे मल वाली कौन सी सर्जिकल इमरजेंसी बच्चों में देखी जाती है?
e: Intussusception occurs when one part of the intestine slides into another, causing obstruction and bloody mucus in stools. [cite: 201]

q: What is the immediate nursing action for a child having a 'Febrile Seizure'? / 'फेब्राइल सीजर' (बुखार वाले दौरे) के दौरान नर्स की तत्काल कार्रवाई क्या है?
o: Ensure safety and side-lying position | सुरक्षा और साइड-लाइंग पोजीशन
e: Protecting the child from injury and maintaining a patent airway is the priority during a seizure. [cite: 110, 201]

q: Which vitamin is given at birth to prevent 'Hemorrhagic Disease' of the newborn? / नवजात के 'रक्तस्रावी रोग' को रोकने के लिए जन्म के समय कौन सा विटामिन दिया जाता है?
e: Newborns have low vitamin K levels; an injection is given to ensure proper blood clotting. [cite: 176]

q: In a child with 'Dehydration', which sign indicates 'Severe' dehydration? / 'निर्जलीकरण' (Dehydration) वाले बच्चे में कौन सा लक्षण 'गंभीर' स्थिति को दर्शाता है?
o: Sunken fontanelle and skin tenting | धंसा हुआ फोंटानेल
e: Severe dehydration is a medical emergency that leads to lethargy and electrolyte imbalance. [cite: 120, 201]

q: What is the primary treatment for 'Physiological Jaundice' in newborns? / नवजात शिशुओं में 'शारीरिक पीलिया' का प्राथमिक उपचार क्या है?
e: Increased feeding promotes bowel movements, helping the baby excrete bilirubin. [cite: 197, 200]

q: What is 'Cleft Lip'? / 'क्लिफ्ट लिप' (Cleft Lip) क्या है?
o: A congenital split in the upper lip | ऊपरी होंठ में जन्मजात दरार
e: It is a congenital anomaly that requires surgical repair and specialized feeding techniques. [cite: 199]

q: Which positioning is best for an infant with 'Gastroesophageal Reflux' (GER)? / 'जीईआर' (GER) वाले शिशु के लिए कौन सी पोजीशन सबसे अच्छी है?
o: Upright position during and after feeding | दूध पिलाने के बाद सीधा रखना
e: Keeping the baby upright helps gravity prevent stomach contents from flowing back into the esophagus. [cite: 201]

q: What does a 'Positive Mantoux Test' in a child indicate? / बच्चे में 'सकारात्मक मोंटू टेस्ट' क्या दर्शाता है?
o: Exposure to Mycobacterium tuberculosis | टीबी के बैक्टीरिया का संपर्क
e: It shows that the child's immune system has encountered TB bacteria, but further tests are needed for diagnosis. [cite: 128, 135]

q: What is the primary role of 'Oral Rehydration Salts' (ORS)? / 'ओरल रीहाइड्रेशन साल्ट्स' (ORS) की प्राथमिक भूमिका क्या है?
o: Replacing lost fluids and electrolytes | तरल और इलेक्ट्रोलाइट्स की पूर्ति
e: ORS is essential for preventing death from dehydration during diarrheal diseases. [cite: 120, 201]

q: At what age is the first dose of 'BCG' vaccine typically administered? / 'BCG' टीके की पहली खुराक आमतौर पर किस उम्र में दी जाती है?
e: BCG is given at birth to protect the newborn against tuberculosis. [cite: 195, 196]

q: Which vaccine is given via the 'Oral' route? / कौन सा टीका 'मौखिक' (Oral) मार्ग से दिया जाता है?
e: OPV is administered as drops into the mouth to prevent poliomyelitis. [cite: 195]

q: What is the recommended age for the first dose of 'Measles-Rubella' (MR) vaccine? / 'मिजल्स-रुबेला' (MR) टीके की पहली खुराक के लिए अनुशंसित उम्र क्या है?
e: The first dose of MR is typically given between 9 to 12 completed months. [cite: 195]

q: Which vaccine is included in the 'Pentavalent' vaccine? / 'पेंटावेलेंट' टीके में कौन सा टीका शामिल है?
o: DPT + Hep B + Hib | डीपीटी + हेप बी + हिब
e: Pentavalent protects against Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus, Hepatitis B, and Hib. [cite: 195]

q: What is 'Phototherapy' used for in newborns? / नवजात शिशुओं में 'फोटोथेरेपी' का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
e: Phototherapy helps break down bilirubin in the skin so it can be excreted. [cite: 197]

q: Which mineral deficiency is the most common cause of 'Anemia' in toddlers? / छोटे बच्चों में 'एनीमिया' का सबसे सामान्य कारण किस खनिज की कमी है?
e: Iron deficiency anemia is prevalent in children due to rapid growth and poor dietary intake. [cite: 15, 57]

q: At what age should 'Complementary Feeding' (Weaning) begin? / 'पूरक आहार' (Complementary Feeding) किस उम्र में शुरू होना चाहिए?
e: After 6 months, breast milk alone is not sufficient, and solid/semi-solid foods should be introduced. [cite: 200]

q: What is the 'Apgar Score' used to assess? / 'Apgar स्कोर' का उपयोग किसके मूल्यांकन के लिए किया जाता है?
o: Immediate physical condition of a newborn | नवजात की तत्काल स्थिति
e: It assesses heart rate, respiratory effort, muscle tone, reflex, and color at 1 and 5 minutes after birth. [cite: 175, 176, 196]

q: Which condition is a medical emergency characterized by a 'Barking Cough'? / 'भौंकने वाली खांसी' (Barking Cough) किस आपातकालीन स्थिति की विशेषता है?
e: Croup is an upper airway infection that blocks breathing and has a distinctive barking cough. [cite: 60, 201]

q: What is the primary role of a 'Child Guidance Clinic'? / 'चाइल्ड गाइडेंस क्लिनिक' की प्राथमिक भूमिका क्या है?
o: Treating behavioral and emotional problems in children | व्यवहार संबंधी समस्याओं का उपचार
e: These clinics provide specialized help for children with developmental or psychological issues. [cite: 195]

q: What does the 'A' in APGAR score stand for? / APGAR स्कोर में 'A' का क्या अर्थ है?
e: Appearance assesses the skin color, ranging from blue/pale (0) to completely pink (2).

q: At what intervals is the APGAR score typically assessed after birth? / जन्म के बाद आमतौर पर किन अंतरालों पर APGAR स्कोर का मूल्यांकन किया जाता है?
e: The 1-minute score determines how the baby tolerated birth, and the 5-minute score tells how the baby is doing outside the womb.

q: A newborn has a heart rate of 110 bpm, a strong cry, and some flexion of extremities. What is the APGAR score for heart rate? / एक नवजात की हृदय गति 110 bpm है, वह तेजी से रो रहा है। हृदय गति के लिए APGAR स्कोर क्या है?
e: A heart rate over 100 beats per minute is assigned a maximum score of 2.

q: In Neonatal Resuscitation, what is the 'Golden Minute'? / नवजात पुनर्जीवन में 'गोल्डन मिनट' क्या है?
o: First 60 seconds to start ventilation if needed | वेंटिलेशन शुरू करने के लिए पहले 60 सेकंड
Time taken to bath the baby
e: The first minute is critical for establishing breathing in a neonate who does not breathe spontaneously.

q: What is the correct position of the head during 'Bag and Mask' ventilation? / 'बैग और मास्क' वेंटिलेशन के दौरान सिर की सही स्थिति क्या है?
e: The 'sniffing' position aligns the airway for effective ventilation.

q: An APGAR score of 3 indicates that the newborn is in: / 3 का APGAR स्कोर इंगित करता है कि नवजात:
o: Severe distress requiring immediate resuscitation | गंभीर संकट
e: Scores 0-3 indicate severe distress, 4-6 moderate distress, and 7-10 normal transition.

q: Which sign is being assessed under 'Grimace' in the APGAR score? / APGAR स्कोर में 'ग्रिमेस' (Grimace) के तहत किस संकेत का मूल्यांकन किया जाता है?
o: Reflex irritability (Response to stimulation) | रिफ्लेक्स चिड़चिड़ापन
e: Grimace evaluates the baby's response to stimulation, such as a flick to the sole of the foot.

q: When using a Bag-Valve-Mask (Ambu Bag) on a neonate, what is the recommended ventilation rate? / नवजात शिशु पर बैग-वाल्व-मास्क का उपयोग करते समय अनुशंसित वेंटिलेशन दर क्या है?
e: A rate of 40-60 breaths per minute mimics the normal respiratory rate of a newborn.

q: What is the primary indicator of successful 'Bag and Mask' ventilation? / सफल 'बैग और मास्क' वेंटिलेशन का प्राथमिक संकेतक क्या है?
o: Visible chest rise | छाती का ऊपर उठना
e: Observation of the chest rising with each breath is the best sign that air is entering the lungs.

q: If the newborn's heart rate remains below 60 bpm despite adequate ventilation, what is the next step? / यदि पर्याप्त वेंटिलेशन के बावजूद नवजात की हृदय गति 60 bpm से नीचे रहती है, तो अगला कदम क्या है?
o: Start chest compressions | चेस्ट कंप्रेशन शुरू करें
e: According to NRP guidelines, chest compressions are indicated if the heart rate is below 60 bpm after 30 seconds of effective ventilation.

q: What is 'Menorrhagia' clinically defined as? / 'मेनोरेजिया' (Menorrhagia) को चिकित्सकीय रूप से क्या कहा जाता है?
o: Excessive or prolonged menstrual bleeding | अत्यधिक या लंबा मासिक धर्म
e: It refers to abnormally heavy or prolonged bleeding that interferes with the woman's quality of life.

q: What is the primary symptom of 'Uterine Fibroids'? / 'गर्भाशय फाइब्रॉइड' (Uterine Fibroids) का प्राथमिक लक्षण क्या है?
o: Abnormal uterine bleeding and pelvic pressure | असामान्य रक्तस्राव
e: Fibroids are non-cancerous growths that often cause heavy periods and pelvic pain.

q: In 'Vesico-Vaginal Fistula' (VVF), there is an abnormal opening between: / 'वेसिको-वैजाइनल फिस्टुला' (VVF) में किसके बीच एक असामान्य छेद होता है?
o: Bladder and Vagina | मूत्राशय और योनि
e: VVF causes continuous involuntary leakage of urine into the vagina.

q: What is the most common cause of 'Vaginal Fistula' in developing countries? / विकासशील देशों में 'वैजाइनल फिस्टुला' का सबसे सामान्य कारण क्या है?
e: Prolonged pressure of the fetal head against the mother's tissues during obstructed labour causes tissue death and fistula.

q: Which nursing intervention is crucial for a patient with VVF? / VVF वाले रोगी के लिए कौन सा नर्सिंग हस्तक्षेप महत्वपूर्ण है?
o: Skin care and preventing excoriation due to urine | त्वचा की देखभाल
e: Constant urine leakage can cause severe skin breakdown and emotional distress; hygiene is priority.

q: What is 'Secondary Amenorrhea'? / 'सेकेंडरी अमेनोरिया' (Secondary Amenorrhea) क्या है?
o: Absence of periods for 6 months in a woman who previously had them | मासिक धर्म का बंद होना
e: It can be caused by pregnancy, stress, weight loss, or hormonal disorders like PCOS.

q: Which symptom is a common 'Menopausal' vasomotor symptom? / कौन सा लक्षण एक सामान्य 'रजोनिवृत्ति' (Menopausal) वासोमोटर लक्षण है?
o: Hot flashes and night sweats | हॉट फ्लैशेस
e: Declining estrogen levels lead to sudden feelings of heat and sweating.

q: What is an 'Ovarian Cyst'? / 'ओवेरियन सिस्ट' (Ovarian Cyst) क्या है?
o: A fluid-filled sac on the ovary | अंडाशय पर तरल से भरी थैली
e: Most cysts are functional and harmless, but some can cause pain or require surgery.

q: In 'Recto-Vaginal Fistula' (RVF), what is the characteristic symptom? / 'रेक्टो-वैजाइनल फिस्टुला' (RVF) में विशिष्ट लक्षण क्या है?
o: Passage of gas or feces through the vagina | योनि से मल का निकलना
e: RVF involves an opening between the rectum and the vagina, leading to fecal incontinence via the vagina.

q: What is the primary treatment for 'Large' uterine fibroids causing severe symptoms? / गंभीर लक्षण पैदा करने वाले 'बड़े' गर्भाशय फाइब्रॉइड का प्राथमिक उपचार क्या है?
o: Myomectomy or Hysterectomy | मायोमेक्टोमी या हिस्टेरेक्टॉमी
e: Surgical removal of the fibroid (myomectomy) or the entire uterus (hysterectomy) may be necessary.

q: What is the nurse's priority assessment immediately after a 'C-Section' (Caesarean)? / 'C-Section' के तुरंत बाद नर्स की प्राथमिकता क्या है?
o: Uterine firmness and vaginal bleeding (Lochia) | गर्भाशय की मजबूती और रक्तस्राव
e: Post-op monitoring for hemorrhage and uterine atony is critical after a Caesarean delivery.

q: In 'Forceps Delivery', which maternal complication is a nurse most alert for? / 'फोरसेप्स डिलीवरी' में नर्स किस जटिलता के लिए सबसे अधिक सतर्क रहती है?
o: Perineal and vaginal lacerations | पेरिनियम और योनि में चोट
e: Forceps can cause significant soft tissue trauma or tears to the mother's birth canal.

q: Why is it essential to examine the 'Placenta' after delivery? / प्रसव के बाद 'प्लेसेंटा' की जांच करना क्यों आवश्यक है?
o: To ensure no fragments are left inside (preventing PPH) | कोई टुकड़ा अंदर न बचा हो
e: Retained placental fragments can prevent uterine contraction, leading to severe Postpartum Hemorrhage (PPH).

q: Which clinical sign indicates 'Vacuum Extraction' may have caused a scalp injury to the neonate? / कौन सा नैदानिक संकेत बताता है कि 'वैक्यूम एक्सट्रैक्शन' से नवजात के सिर पर चोट लगी है?
o: Chignon or Caput Succedaneum | चिग्नॉन या कपूत सक्सीडेनियम
e: Vacuum extraction often causes a localized swelling (chignon) on the baby's head where the suction cup was applied.

q: A nurse is caring for a post-C-section patient. Which symptom should be reported as a possible 'Infection'? / C-section के बाद किस लक्षण को संभावित 'संक्रमण' के रूप में रिपोर्ट किया जाना चाहिए?
o: Foul-smelling lochia and high fever | दुर्गंधयुक्त लोचिया और तेज बुखार
e: These are classic signs of endometritis or wound infection following surgery.

q: What is the primary indication for using 'Vacuum' or 'Forceps' during labour? / प्रसव के दौरान 'वैक्यूम' या 'फोरसेप्स' का उपयोग करने का प्राथमिक संकेत क्या है?
o: Prolonged second stage or fetal distress | प्रसव का लंबा दूसरा चरण
e: These tools are used to assist delivery when the mother is exhausted or the baby needs immediate birth.

q: After a C-section, the nurse must encourage early 'Ambulation' to prevent: / C-section के बाद, नर्स को किसकी रोकथाम के लिए 'एम्बुलेशन' (चलना-फिरना) को बढ़ावा देना चाहिए?
e: Early movement improves circulation and prevents blood clots in the legs after surgery.

q: How many 'Vessels' should a normal Umbilical Cord have during examination? / जांच के दौरान एक सामान्य गर्भनाल में कितनी 'वाहिकाएं' (Vessels) होनी चाहिए?
o: Two arteries, one vein | दो धमनियां, एक शिरा
e: Presence of only two vessels (one artery, one vein) may indicate congenital anomalies.

q: What is 'Retained Placenta'? / 'रिटेंड प्लेसेंटा' (Retained Placenta) क्या है?
o: Placenta not delivered within 30 mins after birth | 30 मिनट तक प्लेसेंटा न निकलना
e: It is a complication that often requires manual removal under anesthesia.

q: Which nerve injury can occur to the newborn in a difficult Forceps delivery? / एक कठिन फोरसेप्स डिलीवरी में नवजात शिशु को कौन सी तंत्रिका क्षति हो सकती है?
o: Facial nerve | चेहरे की तंत्रिका (Facial nerve)
e: Pressure from the forceps blades can cause temporary or permanent facial palsy in the neonate.

q: What is 'Precipitate Labour'? / 'प्रेसिपिटेट लेबर' (Precipitate Labour) क्या है?
o: Labour lasting less than 3 hours | 3 घंटे से कम समय का प्रसव
e: Rapid delivery increases the risk of maternal lacerations and fetal intracranial hemorrhage.

q: Which manoeuvre is used to deliver the head in a 'Breech' presentation? / 'ब्रीच' प्रेजेंटेशन में सिर निकालने के लिए किस पैंतरे (manoeuvre) का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: This specific technique helps in the safe delivery of the after-coming head in breech births.

q: What is 'Lochia Serosa'? / 'लोचिया सेरोसा' (Lochia Serosa) क्या है?
e: It follows lochia rubra and contains serum, leukocytes, and tissue debris.

q: Which vitamin is essential for the prevention of 'Neural Tube Defects' (NTD)? / 'न्यूरल ट्यूब डिफेक्ट' (NTD) की रोकथाम के लिए कौन सा विटामिन आवश्यक है?
e: Folic acid supplementation is recommended before and during early pregnancy to prevent NTDs like spina bifida.

q: What is 'Mastitis'? / 'मास्टिटिस' (Mastitis) क्या है?
o: Inflammation/infection of the breast tissue | स्तन ऊतक की सूजन
e: It often occurs during breastfeeding due to a blocked duct or bacteria entering via a cracked nipple.

q: Which clinical sign indicates 'Atonic' Postpartum Hemorrhage? / 'एटोनिक' प्रसवोत्तर रक्तस्राव का कौन सा नैदानिक संकेत है?
o: Soft, boggy uterus | नरम और ढीला गर्भाशय
e: A soft or boggy uterus fails to compress blood vessels after placental delivery, leading to heavy bleeding.

q: The surgical removal of the 'Uterus' is called: / 'गर्भाशय' को शल्य चिकित्सा द्वारा निकालने को क्या कहा जाता है?
e: Hysterectomy can be total or partial depending on the condition (fibroids, cancer, etc.).

q: What is 'Dysmenorrhea'? / 'डिस्मेनोरिया' (Dysmenorrhea) क्या है?
e: It is characterized by cramping pain in the lower abdomen during or before menstruation.

q: Which family planning method is a 'Hormonal' injectable? / परिवार नियोजन की कौन सी विधि 'हार्मोनल' इंजेक्शन है?
e: DMPA is an injectable contraceptive given every 3 months.

q: What is 'Cervical Erosion'? / 'सर्वाइकल इरोजन' (Cervical Erosion) क्या है?
o: Columnar epithelium replaces squamous epithelium on the cervix | गर्भाशय ग्रीवा का घाव
e: It often appears as a red, velvety area on the cervix and can cause vaginal discharge or spotting.

q: What is the primary characteristic of 'Placenta Previa'? / 'प्लेसेंटा प्रीविया' (Placenta Previa) की प्राथमिक विशेषता क्या है?
o: Painless, bright red vaginal bleeding in late pregnancy | दर्द रहित रक्तस्राव
e: In Placenta Previa, the placenta is implanted in the lower uterine segment, causing painless bleeding when the cervix dilates.

q: Which condition involves the premature separation of a normally implanted placenta? / सामान्य रूप से आरोपित प्लेसेंटा के समय से पहले अलग होने की स्थिति को क्या कहा जाता है?
e: Abruptio placentae is a medical emergency characterized by painful bleeding and uterine tenderness.

q: What is the drug of choice for 'Syphilis' during pregnancy? / गर्भावस्था के दौरान 'सिफलिस' के लिए पहली पसंद की दवा कौन सी है?
e: Penicillin is effective in treating maternal syphilis and preventing congenital syphilis in the fetus.

q: A pregnancy that occurs outside the uterine cavity is known as: / गर्भाशय गुहा के बाहर होने वाली गर्भावस्था को क्या कहा जाता है?
e: Most ectopic pregnancies occur in the fallopian tubes and can lead to life-threatening rupture.

q: What is 'Polyhydramnios'? / 'पॉलीहाइड्रामनियोस' (Polyhydramnios) क्या है?
o: Excessive amniotic fluid (more than 2000ml) | अत्यधिक एमनियोटिक द्रव
e: Excessive fluid can lead to complications like preterm labour and cord prolapse.

q: Which test is used to screen for 'Gestational Diabetes Mellitus' (GDM)? / 'जेस्टेशनल डायबिटीज' के लिए किस परीक्षण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: OGTT measures the body's ability to handle glucose during pregnancy.

q: What is the primary risk for a baby born to a 'Diabetic' mother? / 'मधुमेह' वाली माँ से पैदा हुए बच्चे के लिए प्राथमिक जोखिम क्या है?
e: High maternal glucose causes the fetal pancreas to produce excess insulin, leading to low blood sugar after birth.

q: In 'Hyperemesis Gravidarum', the nurse's priority is: / 'हाइपरमेसिस ग्रेविडरम' में नर्स की प्राथमिकता क्या है?
o: Fluid and electrolyte replacement | तरल और इलेक्ट्रोलाइट प्रतिस्थापन
e: Severe vomiting leads to dehydration and ketosis, requiring IV fluids.

q: Which infection is a part of the 'TORCH' screening in pregnancy? / गर्भावस्था में 'TORCH' स्क्रीनिंग में कौन सा संक्रमण शामिल है?
e: TORCH stands for Toxoplasmosis, Other (Syphilis, etc.), Rubella, Cytomegalovirus, and Herpes.

q: What does a 'Bishop Score' assess? / 'बिशप स्कोर' (Bishop Score) क्या मापता है?
o: Readiness of the cervix for induction of labour | प्रसव प्रेरण के लिए तैयारी
e: It evaluates cervical dilation, effacement, consistency, position, and fetal station.

q: What is the duration of the 'Puerperium' period? / 'प्यूपेरियम' (Puerperium) अवधि की अवधि क्या है?
e: It is the period (approx 42 days) following childbirth during which the mother's organs return to their non-pregnant state.

q: Which hormone is primarily responsible for 'Milk Ejection' (let-down reflex)? / 'दूध के स्राव' के लिए मुख्य रूप से कौन सा हार्मोन जिम्मेदार है?
e: Oxytocin causes the contraction of myoepithelial cells in the breast, ejecting milk; Prolactin stimulates production.

q: What is 'Lochia Rubra'? / 'लोचिया रूब्रा' (Lochia Rubra) क्या है?
o: Bright red vaginal discharge for first 1-3 days post-delivery | लाल रंग का स्राव
e: It consists primarily of blood and decidual debris, appearing immediately after delivery.

q: The first milk produced after birth, rich in antibodies, is called: / जन्म के बाद उत्पादित पहला दूध, जो एंटीबॉडी से भरपूर होता है, कहलाता है:
e: Colostrum is essential for providing natural immunity to the newborn.

q: What is the most common cause of 'Postpartum Hemorrhage' (PPH)? / 'प्रसवोत्तर रक्तस्राव' (PPH) का सबसे सामान्य कारण क्या है?
e: Uterine atony (failure of the uterus to contract after delivery) accounts for 80% of PPH cases.

q: Which family planning method is considered 'Permanent' for males? / पुरुषों के लिए परिवार नियोजन की कौन सी विधि 'स्थायी' मानी जाती है?
e: Vasectomy involves cutting or sealing the vas deferens to prevent sperm from entering the semen.

q: What does 'Exclusive Breastfeeding' mean? / 'एक्सक्लूसिव ब्रेस्टफीडिंग' का क्या अर्थ है?
o: Only breast milk for first 6 months | केवल स्तनपान
e: The infant receives only breast milk (no water or other foods) for the first six months of life.

q: What is 'Involution' of the uterus? / गर्भाशय का 'इनवोल्यूशन' (Involution) क्या है?
o: Uterus returning to its normal size post-delivery | सामान्य आकार में लौटना
e: This process is facilitated by oxytocin release during breastfeeding.

q: Which family planning method also provides protection against STIs? / परिवार नियोजन की कौन सी विधि STI से भी सुरक्षा प्रदान करती है?
e: Barrier methods like condoms are the only ones providing dual protection against pregnancy and infections.

q: What is a primary nursing instruction for a mother with 'Engorged' breasts? / 'भारी' (Engorged) स्तनों वाली माँ के लिए प्राथमिक नर्सिंग निर्देश क्या है?
o: Frequent feeding and warm compresses | बार-बार स्तनपान
e: Regular emptying of the breasts through feeding or expression is the best way to relieve engorgement.

q: Which sign is considered a 'Positive' sign of pregnancy? / गर्भावस्था का 'सकारात्मक' (Positive) संकेत कौन सा माना जाता है?
o: Fetal heart sounds heard by doppler | भ्रूण के दिल की धड़कन
e: Fetal heart sounds and ultrasound visualization are definitive (positive) signs, unlike presumptive signs like nausea.

q: What is the primary characteristic of 'Eclampsia' that distinguishes it from Pre-eclampsia? / 'एक्लेम्पसिया' (Eclampsia) की प्राथमिक विशेषता क्या है जो इसे प्री-एक्लेम्पसिया से अलग करती है?
e: The onset of seizures in a woman with pre-eclampsia marks the transition to eclampsia, a life-threatening emergency.

q: In the first stage of labour, what is the main physiological change? / प्रसव के पहले चरण में मुख्य शारीरिक परिवर्तन क्या होता है?
o: Progressive dilation and effacement of the cervix | गर्भाशय ग्रीवा का विस्तार
e: The first stage begins with regular contractions and ends with full cervical dilation (10 cm).

q: What is 'Naegele’s Rule' used for? / 'नेगेल्स रूल' (Naegele’s Rule) का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
o: To calculate the Expected Date of Delivery (EDD) | ईडीडी की गणना
e: EDD is calculated by adding 9 months and 7 days to the first day of the Last Menstrual Period (LMP).

q: Which position is recommended for a pregnant woman to prevent 'Supine Hypotension Syndrome'? / 'सुपाइन हाइपोटेंशन सिंड्रोम' को रोकने के लिए गर्भवती महिला के लिए कौन सी स्थिति अनुशंसित है?
e: The left lateral position removes pressure from the inferior vena cava, improving blood flow to the heart and fetus.

q: What is 'Episiotomy'? / 'एपिसियोटॉमी' (Episiotomy) क्या है?
o: Surgical incision of the perineum to enlarge vaginal opening | पेरिनियम में चीरा
e: It is often performed during the second stage of labour to prevent jagged tears and facilitate delivery.

q: Which medication is the drug of choice for managing seizures in Eclampsia? / एक्लेम्पसिया में दौरे के प्रबंधन के लिए कौन सी दवा पहली पसंद है?
e: Magnesium Sulfate is highly effective in preventing and treating eclamptic seizures.

q: The 'Second Stage' of labour ends with: / प्रसव का 'दूसरा चरण' किसके साथ समाप्त होता है?
o: Birth of the baby | बच्चे का जन्म
e: The second stage is the expulsion stage, starting from full dilation to the delivery of the neonate.

q: What does 'Antenatal Care' (ANC) primarily aim for? / 'एंटी-नेटल केयर' (ANC) का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: Ensuring a safe pregnancy and healthy mother/baby | सुरक्षित गर्भावस्था
e: ANC focuses on regular monitoring during pregnancy to detect and manage potential complications early.

q: Which emergency condition involves the umbilical cord slipping ahead of the baby? / किस आपातकालीन स्थिति में गर्भनाल बच्चे से पहले बाहर आ जाती है?
e: Cord prolapse is a medical emergency because the cord can be compressed, cutting off oxygen to the baby.

q: During 'Gowning', once the nurse is in the sterile gown, which area is considered sterile? / 'गाउनिंग' के दौरान, नर्स द्वारा स्टेराइल गाउन पहनने के बाद, कौन सा क्षेत्र स्टेराइल माना जाता है?
o: Front from chest to waist level and sleeves | छाती से कमर तक का अगला हिस्सा
e: Only the front and sleeves up to the elbow are considered sterile; the back is always unsterile.

q: What is the primary objective of the 'Recovery Room' (PACU)? / 'रिकवरी रूम' (PACU) का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: Intensive monitoring until vital signs are stable after anesthesia | वाइटल स्थिर होने तक निगरानी
e: PACU ensures a safe transition from an anesthetized state to a conscious state.

q: Which instrument is used during 'Intubation' to visualize the vocal cords? / 'इंट्यूबेशन' के दौरान वोकल कॉर्ड्स देखने के लिए किस उपकरण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: A laryngoscope is essential for correct placement of the endotracheal tube in the trachea.

q: What is the 'Closed Gloving' technique? / 'क्लोज्ड ग्लविंग' (Closed Gloving) तकनीक क्या है?
o: Gloving where hands remain inside the gown sleeves until gloves are on | गाउन के अंदर हाथ रखकर ग्लविंग
e: This technique minimizes the risk of contamination from the hands to the outside of the gloves.

q: In an emergency, what is the role of an 'Ambu Bag' (BVM)? / आपात स्थिति में, 'Ambu Bag' (BVM) की क्या भूमिका है?
o: Manual ventilation to provide oxygen | मैन्युअल वेंटिलेशन
e: Bag-Valve-Mask (BVM) is used to provide positive pressure ventilation to a patient not breathing adequately.

q: What is 'Extubation'? / 'एक्सटयूबेशन' (Extubation) क्या है?
o: Removal of an endotracheal tube | एंडोट्रैचियल ट्यूब को निकालना
e: It is done when the patient is stable enough to maintain their own airway and breathing.

q: Which complication is most likely immediately after 'Intubation'? / 'इंट्यूबेशन' के तुरंत बाद किस जटिलता की सबसे अधिक संभावना होती है?
o: Esophageal intubation or Laryngospasm | ग्रासनली इंट्यूबेशन
e: Accidental placement of the tube in the esophagus is a serious risk and must be checked immediately.

q: How does a nurse check the 'Stability' of a patient in the Recovery Room? / रिकवरी रूम में नर्स मरीज की 'स्थिरता' की जांच कैसे करती है?
o: Aldrete Scoring system (Vitals, Activity, Consciousness) | एल्ड्रेट स्कोरिंग
e: The Aldrete score is a standardized tool to determine if a patient is ready for discharge from PACU.

q: Which resuscitation equipment provides high-energy electrical therapy to the heart? / कौन सा रिससिटेशन उपकरण हृदय को उच्च-ऊर्जा विद्युत चिकित्सा प्रदान करता है?
e: It is used to treat life-threatening arrhythmias by 'resetting' the heart's electrical activity.

q: When performing 'Surgical Hand Scrub', where should the hands be kept relative to the elbows? / 'सर्जिकल हैंड स्क्रब' करते समय, हाथों को कोहनियों के सापेक्ष कहाँ रखा जाना चाहिए?
o: Higher than elbows | कोहनियों से ऊपर
e: Hands must be kept above elbows to allow water to drain from the cleanest area (hands) to the less clean area (elbows).

q: What is the hallmark clinical sign of 'ARDS' (Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome)? / 'ARDS' का प्रमुख नैदानिक लक्षण क्या है?
e: Refractory hypoxemia is when blood oxygen remains low even with high concentrations of oxygen therapy.

q: Which positioning is essential for a patient with 'Pleural Effusion' during Thoracentesis? / थोरासेन्टेसिस के दौरान 'प्लूरल एफ्यूजन' वाले रोगी के लिए कौन सी स्थिति आवश्यक है?
o: Sitting upright leaning forward on a bedside table | आगे झुककर बैठना
e: This position expands the intercostal spaces for easier access to the pleural cavity.

q: In 'Liver Cirrhosis', what is the primary cause of Ascites? / 'लिवर सिरोसिस' में, जलोदर (Ascites) का प्राथमिक कारण क्या है?
o: Portal Hypertension and Hypoalbuminemia | पोर्टल हाइपरटेंशन
e: Increased pressure in the portal vein and low albumin levels cause fluid to leak into the abdominal cavity.

q: What is a 'Varicose Vein'? / 'वैरिकोज वेन' (Varicose Vein) क्या है?
o: Dilated, twisted veins with incompetent valves | सूजी हुई और मुड़ी हुई नसें
e: They occur most commonly in the legs due to high pressure and valve failure.

q: A patient with 'Hemophilia A' is deficient in which clotting factor? / 'हीमोफीलिया ए' वाले रोगी में किस क्लॉटिंग फैक्टर की कमी होती है?
e: Hemophilia A is a genetic disorder caused by missing or defective Factor VIII.

q: What is the nurse's priority for a patient in 'Hepatic Coma'? / 'हिपेटिक कोमा' में मरीज के लिए नर्स की प्राथमिकता क्या है?
o: Maintaining airway and monitoring neurological status | वायुमार्ग और मानसिक स्थिति
e: Protecting the airway is vital as the patient has an altered level of consciousness.

q: After a 'Renal Transplant', what is the most critical complication to monitor? / 'रीनल ट्रांसप्लांट' के बाद, निगरानी के लिए सबसे महत्वपूर्ण जटिलता क्या है?
o: Graft rejection and infection | ग्राफ्ट रिजेक्शन और संक्रमण
e: The nurse must monitor for signs like fever, pain over the graft site, and decreased urine output.

q: Which medication is typically avoided in patients with bleeding disorders like Hemophilia? / हीमोफीलिया जैसे रक्तस्राव विकारों वाले रोगियों में आमतौर पर किस दवा से बचा जाता है?
e: Aspirin inhibits platelet aggregation and can worsen bleeding tendencies.

q: What is 'Arteriosclerosis'? / 'आर्टेरियोस्क्लेरोसिस' (Arteriosclerosis) क्या है?
o: Hardening and loss of elasticity of arteries | धमनियों का सख्त होना
e: It restricts blood flow to organs and tissues, often linked to high blood pressure.

q: Leukemia patients are at high risk for infection because: / ल्यूकेमिया के रोगियों को संक्रमण का उच्च जोखिम होता है क्योंकि:
o: They have immature and dysfunctional WBCs | दोषपूर्ण श्वेत रक्त कोशिकाएं
e: While WBC count might be high, the cells are abnormal and cannot fight infections effectively.

q: What is the primary characteristic of 'Uremia' in kidney failure? / गुर्दे की विफलता में 'यूरेमिया' (Uremia) की प्राथमिक विशेषता क्या है?
o: Accumulation of nitrogenous waste in blood | रक्त में नाइट्रोजनयुक्त कचरे का संचय
e: Uremia occurs when kidneys cannot filter out urea and creatinine, leading to systemic toxicity.

q: Which surgery involves making an opening in the abdominal wall for feces to pass? / मल त्याग के लिए पेट की दीवार में छेद करने वाली सर्जरी कौन सी है?
e: A colostomy is performed when the lower part of the colon or rectum is diseased or removed.

q: What is 'Gowning and Gloving' in OT technique? / OT तकनीक में 'गाउनिंग और ग्लविंग' क्या है?
o: Maintaining surgical asepsis during operation | सर्जिकल एसेप्सिस बनाए रखना
e: Sterile gowns and gloves prevent the transfer of microorganisms from the surgical team to the patient.

q: Which symptom is most indicative of 'Appendicitis'? / 'अपेंडिसाइटिस' का सबसे अधिक संकेत देने वाला लक्षण कौन सा है?
o: Rebound tenderness in the right lower quadrant | दाहिने निचले चतुर्थांश में दर्द
e: Pain at McBurney's point that worsens when pressure is released is a classic sign of appendicitis.

q: What is 'Spinal Anesthesia'? / 'स्पाइनल एनेस्थीसिया' (Spinal Anesthesia) क्या है?
o: Injection into the subarachnoid space for regional numbing | क्षेत्रीय सुन्नता
e: It blocks sensation in the lower half of the body for surgeries like C-sections or hernia repair.

q: Why is 'Incentive Spirometry' taught to post-operative patients? / ऑपरेशन के बाद के रोगियों को 'इंसेंटिव स्पाइरोमेट्री' क्यों सिखाई जाती है?
o: To prevent atelectasis (collapsed lung) | फेफड़ों को सिकुड़ने से रोकने के लिए
e: Deep breathing exercises help keep the alveoli open and prevent pneumonia after anesthesia.

q: Which complication is a nurse most alert for during 'Peritoneal Dialysis'? / 'पेरिटोनियल डायलिसिस' के दौरान नर्स किस जटिलता के लिए सबसे अधिक सतर्क रहती है?
e: Infection of the peritoneal cavity is a serious risk due to the repeated introduction of fluid.

q: What is the primary role of a 'Scrub Nurse' during surgery? / सर्जरी के दौरान 'स्क्रब नर्स' की प्राथमिक भूमिका क्या है?
o: Handling sterile instruments to the surgeon | स्टेराइल उपकरण सर्जन को देना
e: The scrub nurse maintains the sterile field and assists the surgeon directly with instruments.

q: What is 'Hematemesis'? / 'हेमेटेमेसिस' (Hematemesis) क्या है?
o: Vomiting of blood | खून की उल्टी
e: It often indicates an upper gastrointestinal bleed, such as from a peptic ulcer.

q: The 'Recovery Room' (PACU) is used for: / 'रिकवरी रूम' (PACU) का उपयोग किसके लिए किया जाता है?
o: Close monitoring until effects of anesthesia wear off | एनेस्थीसिया का असर खत्म होने तक निगरानी
e: Post-Anesthesia Care Unit (PACU) ensures the patient's vitals and airway are stable immediately after surgery.

q: What is the primary objective of 'Barrier Nursing' in a patient with Tuberculosis? / तपेदिक (TB) के रोगी में 'बैरियर नर्सिंग' का प्राथमिक उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: To prevent cross-infection | क्रॉस-संक्रमण को रोकना
e: Barrier nursing is used to prevent the transmission of infectious pathogens from one person to another in clinical settings.

q: Which clinical sign is characteristic of 'Right-sided Heart Failure'? / 'दाएँ पक्ष की हृदय विफलता' (Right-sided Heart Failure) का विशिष्ट नैदानिक लक्षण कौन सा है?
o: Distended jugular veins and peripheral edema | जुगुलर वेंस में सूजन
e: Right-sided failure causes systemic congestion, leading to fluid backup in the veins and swelling in the limbs.

q: In a patient with Pneumonia, what does 'Wheezing' indicate? / निमोनिया के रोगी में 'व्हीजिंग' (Wheezing) क्या दर्शाता है?
o: Narrowing of the airways | वायुमार्ग का संकुचित होना
e: Wheezing is a high-pitched whistling sound caused by air moving through constricted bronchi.

q: What is 'Aneurysm' in the context of vascular diseases? / संवहनी रोगों के संदर्भ में 'एन्यूरिज्म' (Aneurysm) क्या है?
o: Localized dilation or ballooning of an artery | आर्टरी का फूलना
e: An aneurysm is a weakened area in an artery wall that bulges out, posing a risk of rupture.

q: Which medication is commonly used as a 'Rescue Inhaler' for Asthma? / अस्थमा के लिए 'रेस्क्यू इनहेलर' के रूप में आमतौर पर किस दवा का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Salbutamol is a short-acting beta-agonist that rapidly opens the airways during an attack.

q: What is the main danger of 'Deep Vein Thrombosis' (DVT)? / 'डीप वेन थ्रोम्बोसिस' (DVT) का मुख्य खतरा क्या है?
e: A blood clot in the deep veins can break loose and travel to the lungs, which is life-threatening.

q: A patient with anemia often exhibits 'Pallor'. This is best observed in: / एनीमिया वाले रोगी में 'पीलापन' (Pallor) देखा जाता है। इसे सबसे अच्छा कहाँ देखा जा सकता है?
o: Conjunctiva and nail beds | कंजंक्टिवा और नाखून
e: Mucous membranes and nail beds are ideal sites to assess reduced hemoglobin levels visually.

q: Which heart rhythm is considered a medical emergency? / कौन सी हृदय लय (heart rhythm) को मेडिकल इमरजेंसी माना जाता है?
e: VF is a chaotic heart rhythm that stops the heart from pumping blood, requiring immediate defibrillation.

q: What is the significance of the 'Sputum Culture' test? / 'थूक संवर्धन' (Sputum Culture) परीक्षण का क्या महत्व है?
o: To identify the specific bacteria causing infection | विशिष्ट बैक्टीरिया की पहचान
e: It helps in selecting the most effective antibiotic to treat respiratory infections like TB or pneumonia.

q: In Hypertension management, why is 'Sodium' intake restricted? / उच्च रक्तचाप प्रबंधन में 'सोडियम' का सेवन क्यों सीमित किया जाता है?
o: To reduce fluid retention and lower BP | तरल प्रतिधारण और बीपी कम करने के लिए
e: High sodium leads to water retention in the blood vessels, which increases blood pressure.

q: What is the most common cause of 'Peptic Ulcer'? / 'पेप्टिक अल्सर' का सबसे सामान्य कारण क्या है?
o: H. pylori infection | एच. पाइलोरी संक्रमण
e: Helicobacter pylori bacteria weaken the protective lining of the stomach and duodenum[cite: 134].

q: In 'Chronic Kidney Disease', what is the purpose of 'Dialysis'? / 'क्रोनिक किडनी डिजीज' में, 'डायलिसिस' का उद्देश्य क्या है?
o: To remove waste products and excess fluid | अपशिष्ट उत्पादों और अतिरिक्त तरल को निकालना
e: Dialysis acts as an artificial kidney to filter blood when kidneys fail[cite: 139].

q: What is 'Hepatitis'? / 'हेपेटाइटिस' (Hepatitis) क्या है?
o: Inflammation of the liver | लिवर की सूजन
e: Hepatitis is often caused by viruses (A, B, C, D, E) or toxic substances[cite: 134, 135].
